data.name: SystemDesign_DataTierDesc1
data.value: PostgreSQL 17 runs as a Kubernetes StatefulSet with a persistent volume. The database is initialised on first start via an init SQL script that creates four tables: mathematicians (seeded with 10 entries), calculations, transactions, and audit_log. Indexes are created on all created_at columns and mathematicians.name.

data.name: SystemDesign_DataTierDesc2
data.value: Redis 7 runs as a Deployment (ephemeral cache — no persistent volume). It stores primes results keyed by the upper limit with a 24-hour TTL. A postgres-exporter sidecar exposes PostgreSQL statistics (connections, transactions, replication lag) on port 9187 for Prometheus to scrape.

data.name: SystemDesign_ObservabilityDesc1
data.value: Three pillars of observability are covered by the same open-source stack used locally, now running inside the internet-bank Kubernetes namespace alongside the application.

data.name: SystemDesign_ObservabilityDesc2
data.value: Metrics: Prometheus v3 scrapes the api and postgres-exporter every 15 seconds. Custom application metrics track request counts, computation latency, and Redis cache hit/miss ratios per operation. Grafana visualises these with pre-provisioned dashboards.

data.name: SystemDesign_ObservabilityDesc3
data.value: Logs: Promtail runs as a DaemonSet, reading container logs from the Docker socket on each node and forwarding structured JSON to Loki. Log lines are queryable in Grafana using LogQL, correlated with traces via the trace ID injected into every log entry by the OTel agent.

data.name: SystemDesign_ObservabilityDesc4
data.value: Traces: The OTel Java agent auto-instruments all HTTP requests, JDBC calls, and Redis operations. Spans are sent to Tempo over OTLP/gRPC. From Grafana you can jump from a slow request in a dashboard directly to its full distributed trace.

data.name: SystemDesign_InfrastructureDesc1
data.value: The application runs in an Amazon EKS cluster in eu-central-1 (Frankfurt), inside a dedicated internet-bank namespace. Container images are stored in a private Amazon ECR repository.

data.name: SystemDesign_InfrastructureDesc2
data.value: Both the api and web-client Deployments start with three replicas and are covered by a HorizontalPodAutoscaler (HPA). The api HPA scales between 3 and 10 pods, targeting 70% CPU; scale-up adds two pods per minute and scale-down removes one pod per minute with a 5-minute stabilisation window to avoid flapping. The web-client HPA uses the same CPU threshold plus an 80% memory target.

data.name: SystemDesign_InfrastructureDesc3
data.value: PodDisruptionBudgets ensure at least two replicas of each service remain available during voluntary disruptions such as node drains or rolling updates. A NetworkPolicy restricts pod-to-pod traffic to only the paths required by the application.
